**Applicable Regulation & Compliance Requirement:**
Under IEC 61724-1:2021, the string fault on INV 01.01.002 triggering a 4-day dispatch event requires formal performance investigation if the Performance Ratio (PR) sustained below 0.70 over this period; the operator must document PR calculations with temperature correction (PR_T = PR / (1 + γ×(T_cell − 25))) and state measurement uncertainty in the investigation report. Additionally, if this inverter fault caused plant outage exceeding 1 MW for more than 1 hour, NIS2 Directive & KRITIS-DachG oblige Important Entities (≥200 employees or ≥EUR 10M energy-sector turnover) to report the incident to BSI within 24 hours (early warning) and 72 hours (full report); beyondWatt audit logs must capture this event with the MaStR unit ID per BNetz

**Applicable Regulations & Compliance Obligations:**

Under **Redispatch 2.0 (§13/§14 EnWG, BNetzA Festlegung REGENT)** and the **NIS2 Directive / German KRITIS-DachG**, this plant-wide inverter incident must be logged in beyondWatt with redispatch event details (start/end timestamp, requested set-point, actual output, signal origin) and, if outage exceeds 1 MW for >1 hour, reported to BSI within 24 hours (early warning) and 72 hours (full report). The operator must also file an outage report to **BNetzA MaStR** within 30 days if downtime exceeds 72 hours, including the MaStR unit ID and Betriebsstatus. # Compliance Advisory

Under **EEG 2023 §15 (Entschädigung bei Abregelung)** and **Redispatch 2.0 (§13/§14 EnWG)**, the operator must file a curtailment compensation claim with the responsible grid operator (Netzbetreiber) within **12 months** of the incident, supported by documented evidence: curtailment signal timestamps, irradiation data confirming producible energy, and inverter output logs (before/during curtailment). The claim must specify whether compensation is sought under the 95%-revenue rule (EEG §15) or market-value basis (Redispatch 2.0), as the applicable tariff differs. **Applicable Regulation:** EEG 2023 §15 (Entschädigung bei Abregelung) and Redispatch 2.0 (§13/§14 EnWG).

**Required Actions:** The operator must immediately document the curtailment event by recording (a) the exact timestamps of the grid operator's set-point signal and inverter response, (b) irradiation and producible energy data for the 1-day curtailment period, and (c) inverter output readings before, during, and after curtailment. A formal compensation claim must be filed with the responsible Netzbetreiber within 12 months; the claim shall reference the grid operator's signal origin (SMGW or direct RTU) and cite the Marktwert (market-value) compensation due under Redispatch 2.0, not the feed-in tariff.
